name: PyKEEN (Knowledge Graph Embedding Models)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); Max Planck / PyKEEN contributors

performance.notes: PyTorch GPU; training KGE models on large KGs can take hours

agent_readiness.notes: Knowledge graph embedding library. from pykeen.pipeline import pipeline. result = pipeline(dataset='Nations', model='TransE', epochs=100). model = result.model. predictions = result.predict_all(). from pykeen.triples import TriplesFactory. tf = TriplesFactory.from_path('triples.tsv'). Link prediction: result.predict_target(head='Germany', relation='intergovernmental_organizations'). Evaluation: MRR, Hits@K. 40+ models: TransE, RotatE, TransR, DistMult, ComplEx, ConvE, ERMLP. HPO: optuna-based.

gotchas: Training on large KGs (Wikidata, Freebase) requires significant GPU memory
Entity/relation IDs are internal — map back to original labels carefully
Hyperparameter sensitivity: embedding dimension, negative sampling ratio matter
Cold start problem — no embedding for unseen entities
best_when: Entity relationship modeling with TransE, RotatE, DistMult, ComplEx
Benchmarking multiple KGE models on standard datasets (Nations, FB15k-237)
Drug-target or protein-protein interaction prediction via KGs
summary: PyKEEN provides knowledge graph embedding for link prediction. pipeline(dataset='Nations', model='TransE', epochs=100). predict_target(head=, relation=). 40+ models (TransE, RotatE, DistMult, ComplEx). MRR/Hits@K evaluation. HPO via Optuna. MIT by Max Planck. Best for KG completion and drug-target/protein interaction prediction via KGs. GPU needed for large KGs. Cold start limitation.
